subsections.heading: Purpose
subsections.content: The purpose of Task manager is to solve Develop an AI-powered task management system to reduce task completion time by 30% and increase team productivity by 25% within 6 months. The system will serve project managers, team members, and stakeholders by providing automated task assignment, real-time progress tracking, and predictive deadline alerts. This will improve project delivery rates and reduce manual planning efforts while enhancing team collaboration. Success will be measured by task completion rate, project delivery time, and user adoption rate. as defined in the user inputs.

subsections.heading: Intended Audience
subsections.content:
This document is intended for:
End User
Development Team
Project Managers
Stakeholders

subsections.heading: Included in scope:
subsections.content:
User registration and authentication
Project creation and management
Task assignment and tracking
Real-time collaboration and commenting
File sharing and storage
Customizable workflows and pipelines
Automated task reminders and notifications
Reporting and analytics dashboard
Integration with external tools and services
Role-based access control and permissions
Data backup and recovery
System monitoring and logging

subsections.heading: Excluded from scope:
subsections.content:
Hardware manufacturing
Third-party marketing

subsections.heading: Document Conventions
subsections.content:
Standard IEEE formatting
Bold text for UI elements

subsections.heading: References
subsections.content: PCI-DSS: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

subsections.heading: Product Perspective
subsections.content: A standalone Web Application for the Technical sector, as specified in the project identity.

subsections.heading: Product Features
subsections.content:
User registration and authentication
Project creation and management
Task assignment and tracking
Real-time collaboration and commenting
File sharing and storage
Customizable workflows and pipelines
Automated task reminders and notifications
Reporting and analytics dashboard
Integration with external tools and services
Role-based access control and permissions
Data backup and recovery
System monitoring and logging

subsections.heading: End User
subsections.content:
Tech-savvy
Frequent use

subsections.heading: Operating Environment
subsections.content:
Cloud deployment
SQL database
Python backend

subsections.heading: Design and Implementation Constraints
subsections.content:
PCI-DSS compliance
High performance expectation
SQL database preference
Python backend preference
Cloud deployment preference

subsections.heading: User Documentation
subsections.content:
User Manual
API Documentation

subsections.heading: Assumptions:
subsections.content: Users have a stable internet connection

subsections.heading: Dependencies:
subsections.content:
External tools and services integration
Cloud infrastructure for deployment

subsections.heading: User Registration and Authentication
subsections.content: Description: Manages user registration, login, and authentication.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User submits registration form
Response: System creates a new user account and sends a verification email.
Stimulus: User logs in with credentials
Response: System authenticates the user and redirects to the dashboard.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall validate user input data during registration.
The system shall send a verification email to the user's registered email address.
The system shall authenticate user credentials during login.

subsections.heading: Project Creation and Management
subsections.content: Description: Enables users to create, edit, and manage projects.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User submits a new project request
Response: System creates a new project and assigns a unique project ID.
Stimulus: User updates project details
Response: System updates the project information and notifies team members.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store project metadata in the database.
The system shall validate user input data during project creation.
The system shall notify team members of project updates.

subsections.heading: Task Assignment and Tracking
subsections.content: Description: Allows users to assign tasks, track progress, and set deadlines.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User assigns a task to a team member
Response: System updates the task status and notifies the team member.
Stimulus: User updates task progress
Response: System updates the task status and notifies the project manager.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store task metadata in the database.
The system shall validate user input data during task assignment.
The system shall notify team members of task updates.

subsections.heading: Real-time Collaboration and Commenting
subsections.content: Description: Enables real-time collaboration and commenting on projects and tasks.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User posts a comment on a project
Response: System displays the comment in real-time to all users viewing the project.
Stimulus: User uploads a file to a project
Response: System makes the file available for download to all users with access to the project.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store comments and files in the database.
The system shall validate user input data during commenting.
The system shall display comments and files in real-time.

subsections.heading: File Sharing and Storage
subsections.content: Description: Provides file sharing and storage capabilities.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User uploads a file to the system
Response: System stores the file and makes it available for download.
Stimulus: User downloads a file from the system
Response: System retrieves the file and sends it to the user's device.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store files in a secure and scalable storage solution.
The system shall validate user input data during file upload.
The system shall make files available for download to authorized users.

subsections.heading: Customizable Workflows and Pipelines
subsections.content: Description: Allows users to customize workflows and pipelines.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User configures a custom workflow
Response: System updates the workflow configuration and applies it to new projects.
Stimulus: User adds a new stage to a pipeline
Response: System updates the pipeline configuration and applies it to new tasks.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store workflow and pipeline configurations in the database.
The system shall validate user input data during workflow and pipeline configuration.
The system shall apply workflow and pipeline configurations to new projects and tasks.

subsections.heading: Automated Task Reminders and Notifications
subsections.content: Description: Sends automated reminders and notifications for tasks and deadlines.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: System detects an upcoming deadline
Response: System sends a reminder notification to the task assignee.
Stimulus: System detects a task status update
Response: System sends a notification to the project manager and task assignee.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store notification preferences in the database.
The system shall detect upcoming deadlines and task status updates.
The system shall send notifications to users based on their preferences.

subsections.heading: Reporting and Analytics Dashboard
subsections.content: Description: Provides a reporting and analytics dashboard.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User views the reporting dashboard
Response: System displays project and task metrics in real-time.
Stimulus: User filters dashboard data
Response: System updates the dashboard data based on the user's filter criteria.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store project and task metrics in the database.
The system shall display metrics in real-time on the dashboard.
The system shall allow users to filter dashboard data.

subsections.heading: Integration with External Tools and Services
subsections.content: Description: Integrates with external tools and services.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User configures an external tool integration
Response: System updates the integration configuration and applies it to new projects.
Stimulus: System receives data from an external tool
Response: System processes the data and applies it to the relevant project or task.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store integration configurations in the database.
The system shall validate user input data during integration configuration.
The system shall process data from external tools and apply it to projects and tasks.

subsections.heading: Role-based Access Control and Permissions
subsections.content: Description: Manages access control and permissions based on user roles.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: User logs in with a specific role
Response: System applies the role-based access control and permissions.
Stimulus: User attempts to access a restricted resource
Response: System denies access and notifies the user.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store role-based access control and permissions in the database.
The system shall validate user roles and permissions during login.
The system shall deny access to restricted resources based on user roles and permissions.

subsections.heading: Data Backup and Recovery
subsections.content: Description: Provides data backup and recovery capabilities.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: System detects a data loss or corruption
Response: System restores data from a backup.
Stimulus: User requests a data backup
Response: System creates a backup of the data and stores it securely.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store backups of data in a secure and scalable storage solution.
The system shall validate user input data during backup and recovery.
The system shall restore data from backups in case of data loss or corruption.

subsections.heading: System Monitoring and Logging
subsections.content: Description: Monitors system performance and logs events.

subsections.heading: Stimulus/Response Sequences:
subsections.content:
Stimulus: System detects a performance issue
Response: System logs the issue and notifies the system administrator.
Stimulus: User views system logs
Response: System displays the logs in real-time.

subsections.heading: Functional Requirements:
subsections.content:
The system shall store logs of system events in a secure and scalable storage solution.
The system shall validate user input data during log viewing.
The system shall display logs in real-time.

subsections.heading: User Interfaces
subsections.content: The Task Manager web application will provide an intuitive user interface for project managers, team members, and stakeholders to manage tasks, track progress, and collaborate in real-time. The interface will be accessible on standard computer hardware (keyboard, mouse, display) and will support common web browsers.

subsections.heading: Hardware Interfaces
subsections.content: The Task Manager system does not directly interact with any specialized hardware devices. All user interactions are through standard computer hardware (keyboard, mouse, display).

subsections.heading: Software Interfaces
subsections.content: The Task Manager system will integrate with several external software services, including Google OAuth for authentication, SQL database for data storage, and cloud services for deployment and scalability.

subsections.heading: Communication Interfaces
subsections.content: The Task Manager system will communicate with users through standard web protocols (HTTPS) and will support real-time collaboration features using WebSockets.

subsections.heading: Performance Requirements
subsections.content:
The system shall support 1,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.
Rationale: To accommodate expected peak traffic.
The system shall respond to user requests within 2 seconds under normal load.
Rationale: To ensure a responsive user experience.
The system shall handle a minimum of 100 tasks per minute.
Rationale: To meet the task management needs of the users.

subsections.heading: Safety Requirements
subsections.content:
The system shall perform automated daily backups of all user data.
Rationale: To prevent data loss in case of system failure or corruption.
The system shall have a disaster recovery plan in place.
Rationale: To ensure business continuity in case of a disaster.

subsections.heading: Security Requirements
subsections.content:
The system shall implement OAuth 2.0 for user authentication.
Rationale: To provide a secure and industry-standard authentication mechanism.
Data at rest must be encrypted using AES-256 encryption.
Rationale: To protect sensitive user information in the event of a database breach.
The system shall comply with PCI-DSS standards.
Rationale: To ensure secure handling of payment card information.

subsections.heading: Quality Attributes
subsections.content:
The application must achieve 99.9% uptime.
Rationale: To ensure reliability for enterprise-level clients.
The system shall be maintainable with modular architecture and comprehensive documentation.
Rationale: To enable efficient updates and reduce long-term maintenance costs.
The system shall be scalable to accommodate increasing user demand.
Rationale: To ensure the system can grow with the business.

subsections.heading: Technical Terms
subsections.content:
API: Application Programming Interface - a set of protocols and tools for building software applications.
Microservices: An architectural style that structures an application as a collection of loosely coupled services.
Load Balancing: Distribution of network traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server is overwhelmed.
SQL: Structured Query Language - a language for managing and manipulating data in relational database management systems.
Python: A high-level, interpreted programming language used for developing the backend of the system.

subsections.heading: Acronyms and Abbreviations
subsections.content:
NFR: Non-Functional Requirements - operational constraints defining system performance, security, and quality.
AES-256: Advanced Encryption Standard with 256-bit key - a symmetric encryption algorithm for securing data.
OAuth 2.0: Open Authorization 2.0 - an industry-standard protocol for authorization and authentication.
GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation - a regulation in EU law on data protection and privacy.
PCI-DSS: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard - a set of security standards for handling payment card information.

subsections.heading: System Components
subsections.content:
Authentication Service: Component responsible for verifying user credentials and managing access tokens.
Database Layer: Persistent storage layer managing data operations and ensuring data integrity.
Cloud-based Infrastructure: Infrastructure that uses cloud computing to provide scalable and on-demand access to resources.

subsections.heading: Technologies
subsections.content: Cloud Service Providers: Third-party services that provide cloud-based infrastructure for deployment and scaling.

subsections.heading: Performance Metrics
subsections.content:
Concurrent Users: Number of users actively using the system simultaneously without performance degradation.
Response Time: Time elapsed between a user request and the system's response.
Uptime: Percentage of time the system is operational and accessible, typically expressed as 99.9% or similar.

subsections.heading: Security Terms
subsections.content:
Authentication: Process of verifying the identity of a user or system.
Authorization: Process of determining whether a user has access to a particular resource or functionality.
Encryption: Process of converting plaintext data into unreadable ciphertext to protect it from unauthorized access.

subsections.heading: Quality Attributes
subsections.content:
Reliability: Ability of the system to perform its required functions under normal operating conditions.
Scalability: Ability of the system to handle increased load or demand without decreasing performance.
Maintainability: Ease with which the system can be maintained, updated, or modified.

subsections.heading: Assumption 1:
subsections.content:
Users will have a stable internet connection with a minimum bandwidth of 5 Mbps.
Impact: If users have poor internet connectivity, the system's performance may degrade, leading to delays in task completion and reduced user satisfaction.

subsections.heading: Assumption 2:
subsections.content:
The organization will have an existing Active Directory infrastructure for user authentication.
Impact: Without Active Directory integration, the project would require building a custom authentication system, increasing development time by 3-4 weeks and ongoing maintenance overhead.

subsections.heading: Assumption 3:
subsections.content:
Average concurrent users will not exceed 1,000 during the first year of operation.
Impact: If concurrent usage significantly exceeds this threshold, system performance may degrade, requiring emergency infrastructure scaling and potential architecture modifications.

subsections.heading: Assumption 4:
subsections.content:
Business stakeholders will be available for weekly requirement reviews and timely decision-making.
Impact: Lack of stakeholder availability could lead to requirement ambiguities, implementation delays, and potential rework due to misaligned expectations.

subsections.heading: Assumption 5:
subsections.content:
The database server will have at least 500GB of available storage with capacity to expand.
Impact: Insufficient storage capacity would limit data retention capabilities and could cause system failures if storage limits are reached unexpectedly.

subsections.heading: Assumption 6:
subsections.content:
Users possess basic computer literacy and familiarity with web-based applications.
Impact: If users lack basic digital skills, additional training programs and simplified UI/UX would be required, increasing project costs and extending the rollout timeline.

subsections.heading: Assumption 7:
subsections.content:
Regulatory requirements for data privacy (GDPR, CCPA) will remain stable during development.
Impact: Changes in data privacy regulations could necessitate significant architectural changes, additional compliance features, and potential project delays.

subsections.heading: Assumption 8:
subsections.content:
The cloud service provider will maintain the agreed-upon pricing structure for compute and storage resources.
Impact: Significant price increases could exceed budget constraints, requiring migration to alternative providers or optimization of resource usage to reduce costs.

subsections.heading: Assumption 9:
subsections.content:
Mobile users will primarily use devices with screen sizes of 5 inches or larger.
Impact: Supporting smaller screen devices would require additional responsive design work and more extensive mobile testing, increasing development effort.

Software Requirements Specification
for
Task manager
Prepared by Aditya Shukla
Organization: Intuitive
Date Created: 03/02/2026
Table of Contents
[Note: The Table of Contents will be automatically generated when you open this document in Microsoft Word. If it doesn't appear, right-click and select 'Update Field']
1. Introduction
1.1 Purpose
The purpose of Task manager is to solve Develop an AI-powered task management system to reduce task completion time by 30% and increase team productivity by 25% within 6 months. The system will serve project managers, team members, and stakeholders by providing automated task assignment, real-time progress tracking, and predictive deadline alerts. This will improve project delivery rates and reduce manual planning efforts while enhancing team collaboration. Success will be measured by task completion rate, project delivery time, and user adoption rate. as defined in the user inputs.
1.2 Intended Audience
This document is intended for:
End User
Development Team
Project Managers
Stakeholders
1.3 Project Scope
Included in scope:
User registration and authentication
Project creation and management
Task assignment and tracking
Real-time collaboration and commenting
File sharing and storage
Customizable workflows and pipelines
Automated task reminders and notifications
Reporting and analytics dashboard
Integration with external tools and services
Role-based access control and permissions
Data backup and recovery
System monitoring and logging
Excluded from scope:
Hardware manufacturing
Third-party marketing

Standard IEEE formatting
Bold text for UI elements

PCI-DSS: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
2. Overall Description

A standalone Web Application for the Technical sector, as specified in the project identity.

Stimulus: System detects a performance issue
Response: System logs the issue and notifies the system administrator.

Response: System displays the logs in real-time.
Functional Requirements:
The system shall store logs of system events in a secure and scalable storage solution.
The system shall validate user input data during log viewing.
The system shall display logs in real-time.
External Interface Requirements
User Interfaces
The Task Manager web application will provide an intuitive user interface for project managers, team members, and stakeholders to manage tasks, track progress, and collaborate in real-time. The interface will be accessible on standard computer hardware (keyboard, mouse, display) and will support common web browsers.
User Interface Architecture:
Hardware Interfaces
The Task Manager system does not directly interact with any specialized hardware devices. All user interactions are through standard computer hardware (keyboard, mouse, display).
Hardware Interface Architecture:
Software Interfaces
The Task Manager system will integrate with several external software services, including Google OAuth for authentication, SQL database for data storage, and cloud services for deployment and scalability.
Software Interface Architecture:
Communication Interfaces
The Task Manager system will communicate with users through standard web protocols (HTTPS) and will support real-time collaboration features using WebSockets.
Communication Interface Architecture:
5. Non-Functional Requirements
5.1 Performance Requirements
The system shall support 1,000 concurrent users without performance degradation.
Rationale: To accommodate expected peak traffic.
The system shall respond to user requests within 2 seconds under normal load.
Rationale: To ensure a responsive user experience.
The system shall handle a minimum of 100 tasks per minute.
Rationale: To meet the task management needs of the users.
5.2 Safety Requirements
The system shall perform automated daily backups of all user data.
Rationale: To prevent data loss in case of system failure or corruption.
The system shall have a disaster recovery plan in place.
Rationale: To ensure business continuity in case of a disaster.
5.3 Security Requirements
The system shall implement OAuth 2.0 for user authentication.
Rationale: To provide a secure and industry-standard authentication mechanism.
Data at rest must be encrypted using AES-256 encryption.
Rationale: To protect sensitive user information in the event of a database breach.
The system shall comply with PCI-DSS standards.
Rationale: To ensure secure handling of payment card information.
5.4 Quality Attributes
The application must achieve 99.9% uptime.
Rationale: To ensure reliability for enterprise-level clients.
The system shall be maintainable with modular architecture and comprehensive documentation.
Rationale: To enable efficient updates and reduce long-term maintenance costs.
The system shall be scalable to accommodate increasing user demand.
Rationale: To ensure the system can grow with the business.
6. Glossary
6.1 Technical Terms
API: Application Programming Interface - a set of protocols and tools for building software applications.
Microservices: An architectural style that structures an application as a collection of loosely coupled services.
Load Balancing: Distribution of network traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server is overwhelmed.
SQL: Structured Query Language - a language for managing and manipulating data in relational database management systems.
Python: A high-level, interpreted programming language used for developing the backend of the system.
6.2 Acronyms and Abbreviations
NFR: Non-Functional Requirements - operational constraints defining system performance, security, and quality.
AES-256: Advanced Encryption Standard with 256-bit key - a symmetric encryption algorithm for securing data.
OAuth 2.0: Open Authorization 2.0 - an industry-standard protocol for authorization and authentication.
GDPR: General Data Protection Regulation - a regulation in EU law on data protection and privacy.
PCI-DSS: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard - a set of security standards for handling payment card information.
6.3 System Components
Authentication Service: Component responsible for verifying user credentials and managing access tokens.
Database Layer: Persistent storage layer managing data operations and ensuring data integrity.
Cloud-based Infrastructure: Infrastructure that uses cloud computing to provide scalable and on-demand access to resources.
6.4 Technologies
Cloud Service Providers: Third-party services that provide cloud-based infrastructure for deployment and scaling.
6.5 Performance Metrics
Concurrent Users: Number of users actively using the system simultaneously without performance degradation.
Response Time: Time elapsed between a user request and the system's response.
Uptime: Percentage of time the system is operational and accessible, typically expressed as 99.9% or similar.
6.6 Security Terms
Authentication: Process of verifying the identity of a user or system.
Authorization: Process of determining whether a user has access to a particular resource or functionality.
Encryption: Process of converting plaintext data into unreadable ciphertext to protect it from unauthorized access.
6.7 Quality Attributes
Reliability: Ability of the system to perform its required functions under normal operating conditions.
Scalability: Ability of the system to handle increased load or demand without decreasing performance.
Maintainability: Ease with which the system can be maintained, updated, or modified.
7. Assumptions
Assumption 1:
Users will have a stable internet connection with a minimum bandwidth of 5 Mbps.
Impact: If users have poor internet connectivity, the system's performance may degrade, leading to delays in task completion and reduced user satisfaction.
Assumption 2:
The organization will have an existing Active Directory infrastructure for user authentication.
Impact: Without Active Directory integration, the project would require building a custom authentication system, increasing development time by 3-4 weeks and ongoing maintenance overhead.
Assumption 3:
Average concurrent users will not exceed 1,000 during the first year of operation.
Impact: If concurrent usage significantly exceeds this threshold, system performance may degrade, requiring emergency infrastructure scaling and potential architecture modifications.
Assumption 4:
Business stakeholders will be available for weekly requirement reviews and timely decision-making.
Impact: Lack of stakeholder availability could lead to requirement ambiguities, implementation delays, and potential rework due to misaligned expectations.
Assumption 5:
The database server will have at least 500GB of available storage with capacity to expand.
Impact: Insufficient storage capacity would limit data retention capabilities and could cause system failures if storage limits are reached unexpectedly.
Assumption 6:
Users possess basic computer literacy and familiarity with web-based applications.
Impact: If users lack basic digital skills, additional training programs and simplified UI/UX would be required, increasing project costs and extending the rollout timeline.
Assumption 7:
Regulatory requirements for data privacy (GDPR, CCPA) will remain stable during development.
Impact: Changes in data privacy regulations could necessitate significant architectural changes, additional compliance features, and potential project delays.
Assumption 8:
The cloud service provider will maintain the agreed-upon pricing structure for compute and storage resources.
Impact: Significant price increases could exceed budget constraints, requiring migration to alternative providers or optimization of resource usage to reduce costs.
Assumption 9:
Mobile users will primarily use devices with screen sizes of 5 inches or larger.
Impact: Supporting smaller screen devices would require additional responsive design work and more extensive mobile testing, increasing development effort.
